item.title: Shocking mid-flight brawl erupts after takeoff, sending flight back to airport
item.description: Approximately 10 passengers were reportedly involved in the brawl on easyJet flight EZY3352 from Tenerife to Liverpool, forcing the plane to turn back.
item.content:encoded:
A flight was forced to return to the airport after a passenger brawl erupted onboard. 

The disturbing incident took place earlier this week on a flight from Tenerife, in the Canary Islands, en route to Liverpool, United Kingdom, the BBC and other outlets reported.

Approximately 10 passengers were involved in the brawl, which broke out on an easyJet flight soon after the flight departed, the outlet noted.

WHY A BLOWN-OUT PLANE WINDOW NEARLY SUCKED A PASSENGER OUTSIDE AT 16,000 FEET

Police met the 186-passenger aircraft upon its return, and the flight later continued to Liverpool.

An easyJet spokesperson confirmed the incident to Fox News Digital.

The official said the aircraft "returned to Tenerife South Airport after takeoff due to a group of passengers on board behaving disruptively."

The person added, "The flight then continued onto its final destination."

DELTA FLIGHT FORCED TO DIVERT AFTER PASSENGER GOES INTO LABOR AND DELIVERS BABY MIDAIR

The official added that easyJet's cabin and ground crews are trained to quickly assess and respond to onboard disruptions to prevent passenger safety from being compromised.

"We take these incidents very seriously and do not tolerate disruptive behavior toward our staff," the spokesperson added.

"The safety and well-being of customers and crew is always our highest priority."

CLICK HERE FOR MORE LIFESTYLE NEWS

Commenters on an aviation-focused Reddit forum criticized the passengers for brawling aboard the flight.

"Of all the places, these people decide to fight inside the plane and during the flight," one person said.

CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP FOR OUR LIFESTYLE NEWSLETTER

Brits also poked fun at the flight's destination in Liverpool.

 "Of course, it was a flight to the U.K.," a Reddit user said.

"Can’t be the Liverpool lads," another person said sarcastically. 

TEST YOURSELF WITH OUR LATEST LIFESTYLE QUIZ

The easyJet incident is the latest in a string of recent passenger disruptions aboard airplanes.

Fox News Digital previously covered another case of disruptive behavior aboard an aircraft when a passenger allegedly yanked open an emergency exit during a lengthy tarmac delay.

item.title: Trump allies notch redistricting win as judges find no 'smoking gun' to scrap red state map
item.description: A three-judge panel ruled Tennessee's redrawn congressional map can stand for the Aug. 6 primary, rejecting claims it targeted Black voters in Memphis.
item.content:encoded:
A redrawn Tennessee congressional map expected to give Republicans a 9-0 advantage in congressional seats will remain in place for the state's Aug. 6 primary, handing President Donald Trump and his allies another victory as they work to preserve the GOP's House majority ahead of the midterm elections.

A three-judge panel on Thursday denied a request for a preliminary injunction filed by a group of Black Tennessee voters and civil rights organizations, who argued the map intentionally sought to weaken the power of Black voters.

The lawsuit stems from Tennessee lawmakers redrawing the state's congressional map during a special session in May after the Supreme Court's ruling in Louisiana v. Callais, which made it harder to challenge redistricting plans on racial discrimination grounds. The new map split Memphis and Shelby County into three congressional districts, effectively dismantling Tennessee's only majority-Black district. Republican lawmakers said the goal was to create a map that favored Republicans in all nine congressional districts and help preserve the GOP's majority in the U.S. House of Representatives.

TENNESSEE PASSES NEW CONGRESSIONAL MAP LIKELY TO FLIP FINAL DEM SEAT AS PROTESTS ERUPT INSIDE CAPITOL

The judges — two Trump-appointed judges and one appointed by President Barack Obama — concluded the evidence pointed to partisan politics as the driving force behind the redistricting.

"At bottom, Plaintiffs won't succeed on their vote-dilution claim because they haven't presented meaningful evidence that race — not politics — motivated Tennessee's May 2026 map," the judges wrote.

The judges contrasted the case with the Supreme Court's 2017 decision in Cooper v. Harris, where the High Court ruled that there was direct evidence that race influenced the redistricting process.

SUPREME COURT RULES ON KEY VOTING RIGHTS ACT RULE AS REPUBLICANS AND DEMOCRATS WAGE REDISTRICTING WAR

"Plaintiffs' evidence is a far cry from the 'smoking gun' direct evidence in Cooper v. Harris."

As evidence, the plaintiffs pointed to the map itself, which eliminates the state's only majority-minority district; lawmakers' use of census data containing racial demographics; the unusual timing of the mid-decade redistricting; Tennessee's history of racial discrimination; and affidavits from Black Memphis voters who said the new map diluted their voting strength.

"Plaintiffs present no direct evidence, weak circumstantial evidence, and no path for Tennessee to accomplish its political aims without producing the same effect," the judges wrote.

WHERE TRUMP, GOP VS DEMOCRATS REDISTRICTING BATTLE HEADS NEXT IN WAKE OF KEY COURT RULINGS

Amber Sherman, a plaintiff in the lawsuit and an affected voter, called the panel's rejection of the preliminary injunction "painful."

SIGN UP TO GET THE POLITICS NEWSLETTER

"The decisions by this court continue to further the disenfranchisement of Black voters in Memphis," Sherman said in a statement. "We deserve the same opportunity to shape our future as anyone else. When our voting power is diluted, our neighborhoods have a harder time making their voices heard on the issues that shape our daily lives."

While the preliminary injunction was denied, the lawsuit remains ongoing.

Since Trump began urging GOP-led states to pursue mid-decade redistricting, at least eight states have adopted new congressional maps, with Republicans hoping to gain seats in Texas, Florida, Missouri, North Carolina, Ohio, Alabama, Louisiana and Tennessee. Democrats have responded with redistricting efforts in California, Utah and Virginia, although Virginia's new map was later struck down by the state's Supreme Court.

item.title: Rep Burchett unmasks 'deep state' plot to hide UFO truth after two-hour Pentagon meeting
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett claims "deep state" bureaucrats are blocking UFO transparency following a secret two-hour Pentagon meeting on government disclosure.
item.content:encoded:
Rep. Tim Burchett, R-Tenn., disclosed new details about his high-level push for UFO transparency, arguing that unelected bureaucrats are defying public calls for full disclosure.

Speaking on the "Planet Tyrus" podcast, the Tennessee lawmaker recounted his ongoing battle for transparency and warned that Washington’s culture of secrecy undermines public trust.

"I had a meeting at the Pentagon this week on the issue, a very high-level [meeting]. I’m not at liberty to say who was there and what was, but I was there over two hours if that tells you anything," Burchett said.

TIM BURCHETT, UFO WHISTLEBLOWER RECOUNT OMINOUS GOVERNMENT WARNINGS AFTER DEMANDING UFO TRANSPARENCY

"And the disclosure is real. And the problem we’re having right now is the deep state is blocking a lot of this," he added.

Burchett, who has previously spoken about reviewing classified photos and videos, blamed unelected defense officials for stonewalling congressional oversight. He noted that staffers have claimed executive leadership is kept in the dark.

"I'm in a security hearing, and a little punk with a man bun tells me that the president's on a need-to-know basis. Now, what world am I living in?" Burchett said.

"You got power, you got control, you've got arrogance. And really those are the things that run Washington, D.C. And you throw in a little corruption," he added.

LONG-HIDDEN UFO INFORMATION AT CENTER OF TRUMP PUSH TO FREE FORMER OFFICIALS

Burchett told Tyrus he had spoken to pilots and service members who witnessed unidentified objects but intentionally destroyed footage to avoid professional retaliation. He added that those who reported such encounters underwent an eight-hour debriefing that became part of their personnel record.

FBI LEADING PUSH TO UNCOVER TRUTH ABOUT MYSTERY OBJECTS NEAR US NUCLEAR FACILITIES, FILMMAKER SAYS

"They had psych evaluations because of it. And they would, they could get pulled from the flight line," he said. "And that's just wrong, man. That's a stigmatizing of anybody. And also it's a cover-up of what the heck's going on. And government does not have the right to decide what I can and cannot handle."

Earlier this month, the administration released its fourth batch of declassified unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP) records. The documents include military infrared videos and details about a 2015 unidentified object reported near the nation's primary nuclear weapons assembly facility.

The release is part of a continued push to expand public access to UAP records while maintaining necessary redactions to protect sensitive military details. In February, Trump posted about his directive to agencies to begin the disclosure process for information related to UAPs and extraterrestrial life. 

Trump wrote on Truth Social: "Based on the tremendous interest shown, I will be directing the Secretary of War, and other relevant Departments and Agencies, to begin the process of identifying and releasing Government files related to alien and extraterrestrial life, unidentified aerial phenomena (UAP), and unidentified flying objects (UFOs), and any and all other information connected to these highly complex, but extremely interesting and important, matters. GOD BLESS AMERICA!"

item.title: White House Correspondents' Association president previews upcoming dinner, 'not gonna be intimidated'
item.description: White House Correspondents' Association President Weijia Jiang revealed new security measures for the rescheduled annual dinner set for Friday night.
item.content:encoded:
White House Correspondents’ Association (WHCA) President and CBS News correspondent Weijia Jiang previewed on Thursday what to expect from the rescheduled White House Correspondents' Dinner after an armed suspect stormed the April event.

"I don’t want images of my colleagues hiding under a table to be the last images from this year, of all years," Jiang told People magazine.

She added, "I want us to stand together and to show that we are not gonna be intimidated — that we are going to come back stronger, and we are going to recognize all the people who have done such hard work."

WHITE HOUSE CORRESPONDENTS' ASSOCIATION PRESIDENT ANNOUNCES EVENT BEING RESCHEDULED AFTER SHOOTING

Since then, Jiang described several security measures taken ahead of Friday's dinner in partnership with the Secret Service and a private security firm hired by the WHCA. These measures include moving the location from the Washington Hilton to the Waldorf Astoria ballroom, shrinking the guest list from 2600 attendees to approximately 680 and allowing only one access point through a private entrance.

"I do feel very good about the measures that we’ve taken and really thought through," Jiang said.

Despite the tight schedule to put together a new dinner, Jiang emphasized the importance of celebrating the press during America's 250th anniversary.

TRUMP PRAISES PRESS AFTER WHCD SHOOTING, SAYS UNITY AT DINNER WAS 'BEAUTIFUL'

"Maybe if it wasn’t this year, 2026, it would’ve been easier just to say, ‘Let’s just wait until next year’s dinner,’" Jiang said. "But especially at that time leading up to July 4th, all the country was talking about, all Washington was talking about, was America 250. And the First Amendment is such an important part of that fabric that this year of all years, we have to show the importance of recognizing those freedoms in the First Amendment."

The dinner is largely expected to feature the same speakers and performers scheduled for the original event, such as mentalist Oz Pearlman and President Donald Trump, marking his first time attending the dinner as president.

In addition to honoring journalists and scholarship recipients, the WHCA will award the Secret Service member who survived the April shooting the service award for his efforts. Jiang revealed that the original Washington Hilton staff will also be honored at the dinner.

Though Trump is expected to appear, not all the members of his administration will make a reappearance. First lady Melania Trump, Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio will not be there due to prior commitments.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE COVERAGE OF MEDIA AND CULTURE

Trump's appearance at the White House Correspondents' Dinner has drawn backlash from journalists who demanded that WHCA members use their position to "speak truth to power" against the president.

Jiang emphasized that the dinner would be an effort to "normalize relations" between the Trump administration and journalists rather than exacerbate tensions.

"The Correspondents’ Dinner is our most visible event, but we do so much all year round," Jiang said. "We’ve really been able to course correct, because there was so much friction last year between our association and the administration. And my goal coming back was just to normalize relations so that we could find compromises so that we could work together."

The White House Correspondents' Dinner will take place on Friday.

item.title: ‘Marvelous Mrs. Maisel’ set decorator slain, husband found dead in family already scarred by home killing
item.description: Mary Golding, a 34-year-old set decorator on "The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel," was found stabbed in her Queens home in a suspected murder-suicide.
item.content:encoded:
A new mother who worked on "The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel" TV show was found murdered inside her home in Queens, New York, in an apparent murder-suicide, and reports show she married into a family with a violent past.

The NYPD confirmed to Fox News Digital that 34-year-old Mary Golding was pronounced dead on scene after police discovered her with a stab wound in her back just after midnight Tuesday morning.

According to IMDb, Golding was a set decorator who worked on numerous TV shows, including "The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel."

GILGO BEACH SERIAL KILLER REX HEUERMANN KEPT 'DEXTER'-STYLE KILL ROOM BUT WASN'T AS SMART AS HE THOUGHT: DA

Golding also worked on set design for "Gone Girls: The Long Island Serial Killer," "Goosebumps," "Mr. and Mrs. Smith," and "Hawkeye," among several other shows.

The victim appears to have attended New York University, according to her Facebook profile.

FOLLOW US ON X

Following their discovery of Golding's body, investigators made another gruesome find inside Astoria Park.

FOLLOW THE FOX TRUE CRIME TEAM ON X

The body of Golding's husband was seen hanging from a tree in what appears to be a possible murder-suicide, according to the New York Post.

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST TRUE CRIME NEWS

Sources told the Post that Golding's husband, Joseph Azzaretto, 33, confessed to the killing over the phone when he made calls to one of his wife's friends and a family member.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE U.S. NEWS

The friend, who was not named, found the young mother dead inside the home and found the couple's baby nearby in the home.

LIKE WHAT YOU'RE READING? CLICK HERE FOR MORE

LISTEN TO THE NEW 'CRIME & JUSTICE WITH DONNA ROTUNNO' PODCAST

Prior to the recent killing, Azzaretto's brother, Nicholas, was accused of shooting and killing their father, Frank Azzaretto, in Monroe County, Pennsylvania.

GET BREAKING NEWS ALERTS

GET BREAKING NEWS BY EMAIL

He was reportedly shot in the chest right in front of his wife during a family dispute inside the couple's home in February 2020, the Post reported.

Nicholas Azzaretto pleaded guilty to involuntary manslaughter in November 2021 and was sentenced to 10 years probation, and two years of electronic monitoring, according to the outlet.

SEND US A TIP

Fox News Digital has reached out to Amazon, which streams "The Marvelous Mrs. Maisel."

item.title: Abdul El-Sayed scores Sen Tina Smith's endorsement in Democratic primary that exposes party divide
item.description: U.S. Sen. Tina Smith, a Democrat from Minnesota, has thrown her support behind Michigan Democratic U.S. Senate primary candidate Abdul El-Sayed.
item.content:encoded:
Sen. Tina Smith, D-Minn., has endorsed Democratic Michigan U.S. Senate primary candidate Abdul El-Sayed, describing him as "a fighter."

El-Sayed expressed his gratitude Thursday for the senator's backing.

"Senator Tina Smith has been a fighter for working people in the US Senate," he said in a statement. "She’s fought for and delivered on an economy that works for all of us. I hope to build on her leadership on women’s health and reproductive rights and access which has paved the way for the future I want for my daughters and every American. I’m deeply grateful for her support."

TOP DEM HOPEFUL FLIPS SCRIPT AFTER YEARS IN SOCIALIST ORBIT WITH PUZZLING CLAIM

Sens. Bernie Sanders, I-Vt., Elizabeth Warren, D-Mass., and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y., are some of the figures who have endorsed El-Sayed.

"In all due respect to Haley Stevens, everybody knows that this is not an election between her and Abdul," Sanders said during an event last week in support of El-Sayed. "This is an election between Abdul and the billionaire class. That is what this election is about."

The Senate hopeful is running against U.S. Rep. Haley Stevens, D-Mich., in the Aug. 4 Democratic primary contest.

WARREN DEFIES SCHUMER AS DEM CIVIL WAR EXPLODES OVER MUST-WIN SENATE RACE

Sen. Gary Peters, D-Mich., who announced last year that he would not seek re-election, is supporting Stevens to succeed him in the Senate seat.

"Over the last eighteen years, while serving the people of Michigan, I have been surrounded by many extremely talented and dedicated partners who work tirelessly for our state. Haley Stevens has been that partner in Congress," Peters wrote in a statement. "Haley Stevens will be ready on day 1 to fight for Michigan, which is why I am excited to announce I am all in for Haley Stevens."

Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, D-Calif., is also among those backing Stevens.

DEMOCRATS' CIVIL WAR HEADS TO MICHIGAN WHERE PROGRESSIVES FACE BIGGEST TEST YET IN HIGH-STAKES SENATE SHOWDOWN

Former Rep. Mike Rogers, who is backed by President Donald Trump, does not face any competition heading into the Michigan Republican U.S. Senate primary.

item.title: Trump says Iran will pay — what it takes to end the war remains unclear
item.description: Trump's Iran campaign now focuses on reopening the Strait of Hormuz, but no clear conditions exist for ending strikes as nuclear talks stall.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly five months after launching a sweeping campaign against Iran’s nuclear, missile, naval and proxy capabilities, the Trump administration is now fighting to reopen the Strait of Hormuz while negotiations have yet to produce a permanent settlement.

The administration says its renewed strikes have a clear immediate objective: "making Iran pay." Less clear is what Iran must do to end the strikes — or what conditions would allow the U.S. to step back from a growing military commitment in the region.

President Donald Trump has threatened to expand the campaign to Iran’s nuclear sites, bridges, power plants and other infrastructure.

ARMY IDS 2 VICTIMS KILLED IN JORDAN AIR BASE ATTACK; TRUMP: IRAN WILL PAY 'MANY TIMES OVER'

In response to questioning on the end goals in Iran, a U.S. official said: "Right now, the President is focused on making Iran pay for their violations of the MOU and their continued acts of terrorism in the Strait of Hormuz," referring to the memorandum of understanding between Iran and the United States.

"In addition, the president will make Iran pay for the recent deaths of U.S. soldiers," the official said.

"These devastating blows will continue until the president deems otherwise, but talks between our countries are continuing," the official said. "President Trump has all of the cards and all options at his disposal, and Iran can never possess a nuclear weapon."

The response defined the immediate campaign as punishment for Iran’s actions but did not identify any condition Tehran could meet to halt the strikes beyond Trump deciding that the punishment had been sufficient. Follow-up questioning by Fox News Digital went unanswered.

STEVE FORBES URGES TRUMP TO KEEP THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ OPEN AT ALL COSTS

When Trump launched its military Operation Epic Fury mission on February 28, the White House laid out four sweeping objectives: preventing Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon, destroying its ballistic missiles and production capacity, annihilating its navy and severing its support for terrorist proxies.

The administration declared those military objectives achieved in April. But Iran retained enough capability to close the Strait of Hormuz, attack commercial shipping and target U.S. forces and allies across the region.

The administration’s immediate focus is now the strait. 

U.S. Central Command said Thursday that its latest strikes targeted Iranian maritime capabilities, coastal surveillance sites, air defenses and missile and drone facilities to degrade Iran’s ability to attack commercial vessels.

TRUMP SEEKS $672M TO STOP IRANIAN NUKES AS NEGOTIATORS WEIGH FATE OF URANIUM STOCKPILE

Secretary of State Marco Rubio has framed the current campaign as an effort to prevent Iran from establishing a "dangerous precedent" by controlling an international waterway, while maintaining that Washington remains open to diplomacy. He said Iran must stop attacking commercial ships and reopen the strait before the United States changes its behavior.

"Their behavior has to change in order for ours to change," Rubio said Sunday.

Yet recent strikes have expanded beyond Iran's maritime capabilities to include bridges and other infrastructure as Trump continues threatening nuclear facilities, raising new questions about how the widening target set fits into the administration's broader strategy.

Meanwhile, the final nuclear agreement envisioned under the June memorandum of understanding has not materialized, even as the White House says talks between Washington and Iran are continuing. The memorandum had envisioned an agreement within 60 days, but renewed fighting has left its timing and prospects uncertain.

Trump has continued threatening Iran’s nuclear infrastructure, including the deeply buried Pickaxe Mountain facility. But he has also appeared to soften one of the administration’s original demands, saying in June that Iran could retain some ballistic missiles in proportion to neighboring countries.

The administration has not clarified whether the original missile objective was achieved, relaxed as part of negotiations or remains part of a future settlement, nor whether ending Iran’s support for proxies remains a condition for victory.

Publicly, the renewed campaign has a clear immediate military task — protecting shipping and preventing Iran from asserting control over Hormuz — but the connection between that mission, the stalled nuclear process and Trump’s threats to widen the war remains unexplained.

Eighteen U.S. service members have been killed since the war began, while Secretary of War Pete Hegseth told lawmakers Tuesday that the campaign has cost an estimated $37.5 billion.

Retired Air Force Brig. Gen. Robert Spalding, a senior fellow at the Hudson Institute conservative think tank, who served as a senior strategy official in the first Trump White House, argued that the campaign can still be understood around one overriding objective.

"I think the end goal is to prevent them from having a nuclear weapon," Spalding told Fox News Digital. "The goal is very clear. It is: How do you want to go about getting to those goals, and then what are you willing to pay to achieve them? That’s the decision that has to be made."

On Hormuz, Spalding predicted that protecting commercial shipping could require a prolonged U.S. presence, comparing it to Operations Northern and Southern Watch, the yearslong U.S.-led enforcement of no-fly zones over Iraq.

"I don’t think that we’re leaving the area anytime soon," Spalding said.

He described that potential mission as "almost an airborne and seaborne occupation of the strait and the Gulf" intended to preserve the free flow of international shipping.

Such a mission could give the current strikes a defined military purpose, but it would not necessarily resolve the nuclear negotiations, eliminate Iran's remaining leverage or produce a political settlement. It could instead leave American forces enforcing access to Hormuz for an extended period while the broader conflict remains unresolved.

Ali Vaez, Iran project director at the International Crisis Group, said the administration’s changing public emphasis reflects the absence of a coherent strategy connecting its military actions to an achievable end state.

"The administration does not seem to have a clear strategy," Vaez told Fox News Digital. "It’s been a moving goalpost."

Vaez also argued that the conflict has given Iran a potent new form of leverage: the demonstrated ability to choke shipping through Hormuz and impose economic pain on the United States. He described Iran’s control over the strait as a "weapon of mass destruction."

"For more than four decades, the United States has been able to coerce the Iranian regime economically," Vaez said. "The Iranians never had the possibility of retaliating in kind, of putting pressure on the pocketbook of every American citizen."

The U.S. is now trying to inflict enough pain on Iran that it abandons its insistence on controlling the strait, according to Vaez. But Iran views that control as its most valuable remaining source of leverage and one it cannot surrender without weakening its position in any future negotiations, he argued.

"We are in a zero-sum situation," Vaez said. "The U.S. is trying to remove a tool of leverage from the hands of the Iranians that they believe is essential to their survival."

Although the United States retains overwhelming military superiority, Vaez argued that neither side has been able to deliver a decisive blow. Washington can continue inflicting damage, he said, while Tehran has a greater willingness to absorb it because it views the conflict as a struggle for the regime’s survival.

"There is no military solution to resolving the problem in the strait," Vaez said. "After much more death and destruction, I think both sides would return to the same logic that led them to agree to the MOU in the first place — which is that neither side is really able to deliver a knockout blow to the other."

item.title: Jon Bon Jovi abruptly ends Madison Square Garden show after health setback
item.description: Jon Bon Jovi stopped his Madison Square Garden residency show after just 90 minutes due to a health issue, with Train frontman Pat Monahan stepping in to help.
item.content:encoded:
Jon Bon Jovi unexpectedly cut his Madison Square Garden concert short Thursday night after appearing to have difficulty getting through the performance – forcing Train frontman Pat Monahan to step in and help carry the show across the finish line.

A representative for Bon Jovi confirmed to Fox News Digital that the concert ended early, saying the singer told fans from the stage he had been battling a health setback.

"Jon Bon Jovi spoke from stage and told fans he has been battling a sinus infection which led to the show's early ending," the representative said in a statement.

ROD STEWART CANCELS LAS VEGAS CONCERT HOURS BEFORE SHOWTIME ON DOCTOR'S ORDERS

"As part of the Bon Jovi residency at Madison Square Garden, Jon has said numerous times that it has been a joy to return to live shows for the band. Updated information will be available shortly."

An insider who attended the concert told Fox News Digital Bon Jovi performed for roughly 90 minutes.

Bon Jovi's previous Madison Square Garden residency shows have lasted over two hours, the insider noted. Thursday's performance wrapped after approximately 15 songs, ending far earlier than fans had come to expect.

LIKE WHAT YOU’RE READING? CLICK HERE FOR MORE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS

Monahan was in the arena as a fan Thursday night and was not part of the scheduled show, according to the insider.

WATCH: JON BON JOVI ABRUPTLY CUTS MADISON SQUARE GARDEN CONCERT SHORT

The 64-year-old rocker was performing on night eight of his nine-show residency when he stopped the show early. Monahan joined him for "Livin' on a Prayer," taking over the vocals midway through the song.

The shortened performance comes as Bon Jovi continues his comeback following vocal cord surgery in 2022. This is his first major tour since undergoing the career-saving procedure.

Just months before launching the "Forever Tour," Bon Jovi admitted he had been prepared to walk away from music after a vocal cord injury left him fearing he could never perform at the level fans expected.

In October 2025, Bon Jovi recalled realizing something was wrong after a 15-show run.

At the end of the final show, he told his wife he thought it was "pretty good," but his wife disagreed, Bon Jovi recalled on the "How to Fail" podcast.

"I don't think I could do this anymore. I don't know why, but I'm OK with walking away from this because there's no way I'm dragging down the legacy," Bon Jovi said.

CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP FOR THE ENTERTAINMENT NEWSLETTER

Doctors later discovered the source of the problem.

"I sought out a surgeon who explained to me that one of my vocal cords had literally atrophied," Bon Jovi said. "It was dying."

He underwent surgery and received an implant designed to help his vocal cords close properly.

"If I worked hard, he promised me nothing other than I would be better than I was that night in Nashville," Bon Jovi explained.

Still, the road to recovery wasn't easy.

"Had I known that it was going to be a three-and-a-half-year recovery, I very well might have said thank you, good night," he said. "But the process and the progress was steady enough that I didn't lose faith."

By the time he announced the "Forever Tour," Bon Jovi said he felt "close enough to fully recovered" to return to the stage.

"I'm not doing this for the applause. I'm not doing it for the money. I'm not doing it the fame," he said. "I've had enough of all of it.

"But I would like to feel that joy and the resonance, even if it were only one last time. I don't think this is anywhere near a farewell tour or anything like that. But if it were, you could trust that these few shows that I've announced are gonna be joy-filled."

The "Forever Tour" began July 7 with a nine-show residency at Madison Square Garden and is scheduled to wrap Sept. 9 at London's Wembley Stadium.

item.title: Country music's biggest star Morgan Wallen releases 'Been By Now' as his latest hit song
item.description: Morgan Wallen's new single "Been By Now" tells the story of a failing relationship on the brink of collapse and immediately started going viral.
item.content:encoded:
Morgan Wallen released his latest single for fans on Friday.

The country music sensation is the unquestioned king of the genre at the moment, and he appears to show no signs of giving up the throne.

Whether the awards shows want to give him the recognition he's earned or not, his success speaks for itself.

MORGAN WALLEN PULLS OFF INCREDIBLE CAREER ACCOMPLISHMENT AS ANOTHER SONG HITS A BILLION STREAMS ON SPOTIFY

He dominates the charts and his concerts sell out wherever he goes. Now, he has new music for fans to enjoy.

The country music superstar released "Been By Now" early Friday morning for his millions of fans across the globe.

The song tells the story of a failing relationship that is on the brink of all-out collapse, and as expected, it immediately started going viral.

MORGAN WALLEN AND ELLA LANGLEY'S BALTIMORE PERFORMANCE OF 'I CAN'T LOVE YOU ANYMORE' GOES VIRAL

Give it a listen below, and let me know your thoughts at David.Hookstead@outkick.com.

Fans definitely enjoyed the song and showed support for Wallen in the comments.

One fan wrote, "I was so ready for this🎉🎉🎉🎉❤❤❤❤ I love his music."

MORGAN WALLEN AND ELLA LANGLEY HOLD THE TOP TWO ALBUM SPOTS IN AMERICA FOR FIRST HALF OF 2026

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

A second listener added, "Man has never put out a bad song! Not one! Let that sink in! The greatest country artist of all time!"

Greatest of all-time might be a bit much at this point, but there's no question the fans love him and his music.

What do you think of Wallen's latest track? Let me know at David.Hookstead@outkick.com.

item.title: Trump must close the back door China uses to exploit North American trade
item.description: Steel imports from China, South Korea and Vietnam surged through Mexico and Canada, exploiting USMCA loopholes the Trump administration seeks to close.
item.content:encoded:
Review doesn’t mean rubber stamp. The United States shouldn't rush to extend the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement for another 16 years simply because the calendar says it's time.

USMCA has strengthened North American trade and deepened commercial ties with two of the United States’ largest trading partners. But its scheduled review was never intended to be a formality. It was designed to force policymakers to evaluate whether the agreement is delivering on its promises and to correct course when it isn't.

That is exactly what the Trump administration is doing.

TRUMP SHOULD EXPAND HIS BORDER CRACKDOWN. TIMING IS PERFECT TO REIN IN CANADA AND MEXICO

Having just returned from the latest round of negotiations in Mexico, I am encouraged by constructive conversations. Progress has been made. But significant issues remain unresolved. Extending the agreement before addressing them would surrender the United States' greatest source of leverage.

In 2025, Mexico was our nation's largest trading partner, with around $873 billion in goods trade. Yet while trade with the United States has expanded, Mexico has increasingly served as a gateway for steel from countries whose governments distort global markets through subsidies and overcapacity.

Between 2020 and 2024, Mexican steel imports from South Korea, China and Vietnam rose 44%, 59% and 39%, respectively. Overall steel imports into Mexico climbed 45% during that period. Those imports gained preferential access to the North American market despite originating from countries that have long undermined fair competition.

TRUMP JUST EXPANDED HIS TARIFF PLAYBOOK WITH A POWERFUL TRADE WEAPON NO PRESIDENT HAS EVER USED

The consequences are clear in U.S. import data. Imports of Mexican rebar in 2024 were roughly 1,148% above their average level before USMCA. At the same time, demand for steel in the U.S. declined by more than 11 million tons since USMCA was negotiated, yet imports from Mexico remained elevated.

Canada presents a similar challenge.

From 2020 through 2024, Canadian imports of Chinese steel increased approximately 75%. China remains the world's largest source of excess steel capacity, a problem responsible for displacing production, depressing prices and killing manufacturing jobs across the globe. Yet Canada enjoyed privileged access to the U.S. market.

CANADA REPORTEDLY SCRAPS JOINT BRIDGE CELEBRATION WITH US AFTER TRUMP RENEWS TARIFF THREAT

These trends are inconsistent with the spirit of a North American trade agreement intended to strengthen regional manufacturing.

Fortunately, the solutions are straightforward. Canada and Mexico should adopt border measures comparable to the United States’ successful Section 232 steel tariffs to prevent unfairly traded steel from entering North America through alternative routes. Both countries should restrict investment by non-market economies in strategically important industries. USMCA should strengthen steel-related rules of origin and adopt a true "melted and poured" requirement so that steel receiving preferential treatment is genuinely produced in North America, not merely processed here after originating elsewhere. Above all, we cannot allow the hard-won gains in revitalizing American steel manufacturing to be rolled back in ways that export American jobs.

Critics argue that businesses need certainty and that renewing USMCA immediately would provide it. But certainty was never the sole purpose of the agreement's review mechanism. Its architects intentionally built periodic reviews into the agreement to ensure the pact continued serving North American interests.

Nor is there a cliff approaching. Failure to renew this year does not terminate USMCA. The agreement remains in force for years to come unless a country chooses to withdraw, while scheduled reviews provide opportunities to strengthen its provisions.

The review provisions are working exactly as intended.

Negotiations with Mexico are the furthest along, while talks with Canada may intensify after President Trump’s recent deployment of the section 338 tariffs. If the Trump administration secures a strong agreement that meaningfully addresses longstanding concerns with Mexico, it should not hesitate to move forward on a bilateral basis rather than allow the broader USMCA review to become a source of unnecessary delay. Such an agreement could also serve as a blueprint that Canada would be welcome to adopt if and when it is prepared to meet the same standards.

The objective should not be preserving a three-country framework for its own sake, but securing the strongest possible agreement with America’s largest trading partners. If Mexico is prepared to negotiate comprehensive reforms that strengthen North American manufacturing and close the agreement’s most significant loopholes, President Trump should seize that opportunity.

The United States accounts for roughly 85% of North America's economic output. That market access is enormously valuable, and it should be accompanied by reciprocal commitments to combat unfair trade, strengthen regional supply chains and prevent non-market economies from exploiting loopholes in our trade rules.

North America is strongest when its trade is genuinely fair. The current review offers a rare opportunity to modernize USMCA, close obvious loopholes and ensure the agreement fulfills its original purpose. President Trump is right to resist calls for a premature extension. A stronger agreement is worth the wait.

item.title: Roger Goodell floats holding NFL draft in Charlotte and returning for something bigger later
item.description: Roger Goodell told a Charlotte audience the city is "built for a draft" and didn't rule out a future Super Bowl, but hotel rooms remain a key hurdle.
item.content:encoded:
The NFL has become something of a traveling road show, with games played all over the Earth, the draft held throughout the country, and the Super Bowl touring one elite American city after another. And Charlotte may be close to becoming one of the destinations.

NFL commissioner Roger Goodell said Thursday evening during a fireside chat hosted by Carolina Panthers owner David Tepper that he believes Charlotte would lend itself to hosting an NFL draft, and he did not dismiss the idea of the city getting a Super Bowl at some point in the future.

NFL OWNERS UNANIMOUSLY VOTE TO BRING SUPER BOWL LXIV TO NASHVILLE'S NEW $2 BILLION STADIUM IN 2030

"...You see the way the downtown's evolving..." Goodell told an audience of Panthers and local dignitaries gathered to celebrate the renewed naming right for Bank of America Stadium and the facility's renovation. "The first [thing] came into my mind: I said to David, 'This is built for a draft.'

"It's the location of Charlotte in general. And I think the way the draft is now, it's close to a million people attended. And they're coming from all over, including internationally. And it's become a global event that I think would be a terrific thing here in this town, with everything that's being built."

The NFL draft is is spoken for over the next two years. Washington D.C. will hold the event in 2027 and Minneapolis has the event for 2028. So if Charlotte is going to compete for the event, the 2029 draft would be the first realistic target.

The draft has been steadily growing as a fan event since the NFL cast aside the indoor versions, which used to be held in a New York City hotel or Radio City Music Hall. The 2017 draft in Philadelphia was the first held outdoors and it had 205,000 in attendance.

That number is dwarfed by this year's event in Pittsburgh that set a new record with 805,000 people in attendance.

But for all its emerging significance, the draft does not compare on scale to the Super Bowl as the NFL's signature road event.

Tepper would love one in Charlotte even if that's not likely to happen soon.

"There'd just be another step in that," Goodell said. "As far as the Super Bowl is concerned, there is no question in that the city keeps growing the way it is. It's really the stadium and the facility here is wonderful, and it's certainly Super Bowl quality.

"The real issue with Super Bowls now is number of hotel rooms, all the hotels, and other facilities, which, every time I come back here, it's completely different than it was when I was here the last time.

"And so, I think that as growth continues, I think, you know, that's probably somewhere down the line in the future."

The next available Super Bowl is in 2031.

A leaked NFL Super Bowl bid specification sheet from the early 2010s required a Super Bowl host city to have hotel rooms equal to at least 35% of the stadium's seating capacity within a 60-minute drive – which equals about 25,000 or so rooms for a typical NFL stadium.

But as the game has grown, the required number of rooms has also grown to an estimated 60,000 to 75,000.

The greater Charlotte area boasts approximately 50,000 hotel rooms but many of those are in outlying areas.

Tepper remains hopeful.

"It would be great to get into the Super Bowl here. It might be great if we had a Super Bowl here," he said. "We have, I heard we have, we're the center of the east coast, with one of the best transportations, sixth busiest airport in the country.

"And I was mentioning in the way back there, that I had noticed like in Augusta, we have more million-dollar house probably closer to the stadium than any other stadium in the country. And I notice in Augusta, they give those homes up to the extent that we may not have all the luxury hotels.

"In fact, I got Dan Morgan's house for you already."

Dan Morgan is the President of Football Operations and General Manager for the Panthers. And, yes, Tepper was kidding."

Follow Armando Salguero on X: @ArmandoSalguero

item.title: Why the Chicago White Sox moneyline is the sharp betting play against the Houston Astros today
item.description: Sharp money and line movement favor the Astros, but Davis Martin's 1.80 home ERA makes the Chicago White Sox moneyline an intriguing betting play.
item.content:encoded:
I had a play last night on the Royals and Tigers game, and to spoil any surprise, it was a loser. I took the Royals at +170 mostly because I felt like the line was way off. I still cashed the under, but I hope anyone who is serious about sports betting takes a look at that article. Despite it being a loss, I loved the play. There was a ton of value on the Royals, who lost 4-3. There are times you need to take shots, and this one rewarded me mentally, because I feel like I at least put myself in the right spot to make money. I'm hoping to actually get the money, physically, in today's Astros and White Sox game.

The Houston Astros are still hanging in there, but the question if they are going to buy, sell, or stand pat in the next week has to be considered. For the season, they are four games under .500, but they are just two games back of the American League West division. That's not exactly the position to be in if you plan to throw in the towel. Their best player, Yordan Alvarez, is having an MVP season, yet another reason to keep pushing and see if they can make the postseason.

They did trade away one of their starters already, but I don't feel like that was a sign of the team trying to give up. I think it was more about their roster and salary than anything. Today, they are putting out Spencer Arrighetti on the mound. For the season, Arrighetti is 7-5 with a 4.34 ERA and a 1.31 WHIP. He has been worse on the road with a 5.52 ERA. He also has allowed eight earned runs in both of his past two road starts. I'm thinking he might be coming out of whatever issues he had in June, though. In May, he had a 0.93 ERA, and in June he had a 9.00 ERA. So far, through three starts, he has allowed eight earned runs in four innings, and two earned runs in 11 innings covering two starts.

The Chicago White Sox were expected to be sellers, but this team has some heart and looks like they are piecing things together. Part of the reason sports are so fun is teams like the White Sox. A team that no one expected anything from this year, and they've turned it around to be in a playoff race. There is still a long way to go, and one injury could derail the whole thing, but you have to feel solid about how they've played and looked if you're a Sox fan.

One of the brightest spots on this roster has been today's starter, Davis Martin. For the season, Martin is 9-5 for the year with a 3.31 ERA and a 1.27 WHIP. Martin has been elite at Guaranteed Rate Field this season with a 1.80 ERA. He has only allowed nine earned runs at home in 45 innings. And, five of those earned runs came in the last start against the Red Sox. Perhaps most impressive is that he has allowed just 33 walks all year, and only 10 have come at home.

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

Baseball is a sport in which you usually do have the opportunity to follow the sharps and line movement fairly confidently. There aren't a ton of public bettors loading up on teams. Sharp money tends to move these lines, and the line is moving in favor of the Astros. However, sharps don't always win either. There is also a move where they will bet on one side, just to lower the line and get bigger money on the opposite side. I could see that happening here.

If you don't understand what that means, no worries. Basically, I think the White Sox are going to win this game. Martin is a better pitcher, in better form, and more reliable as a home starter. Arrighetti is bad on the road, and something has been wrong with him since June. It is possible that sharps saw some good value in the Astros, but now that the line is a lot lower, I think the Sox are the right side and could see money poured into them late. Give me the White Sox on the moneyline.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

For more sports betting information and plays, follow David on X/Twitter: @futureprez2024

item.title: LeBron James makes NBA free agency decision, announces plans to sign with 76ers
item.description: NBA legend LeBron James announced on social media that he will be joining the Philadelphia 76ers, who will the fourth team he has played for in his career.
item.content:encoded:
LeBron James announced Friday that he will be joining the Philadelphia 76ers next season.

James, 41, is reportedly signing a two-year, $8 million deal, taking a major discount, to join the 76ers, ESPN first reported.

James announced his decision in a post on X where he said he thought he was done at the end of last season and that he had likely played his final game. 

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

"I thought I was done when the season ended. I wasn't ready to announce it, and I knew I needed some time to really decide, but I was pretty sure I played my last game. I was honest at that last press conference when I said I needed to look at myself and decide if I still love this game," James wrote on X.

"I still truly love this game, and I have more to give."

James called the last few weeks "special," saying he has never before been able to take this much time to think through such an important decision.

"I’ve had an incredible few months with all the people I love trying to figure it all out. This is my last decision. I’m not going for money. I’m not going for family. What am I really playing for at this point? I still want to sacrifice. I still want to work. I still want to grind. I still want to compete, to win and to have a chance at the feeling of winning," James wrote. 

ZERO BS. JUST DAKICH. TAKE THE DON'T @ ME PODCAST ON THE ROAD. DOWNLOAD NOW!

The NBA legend said he still wants to sacrifice and work to try to win another NBA title. 

"I still want to sacrifice. I still want to work. I still want to grind. I still want to compete, to win and to have a chance at the feeling of winning another championship," James said. "I believe I can help make the Philadelphia 76ers a championship team and I am so excited to energize a new fan base and start this incredible journey one last time. Thank you LA. Miami I’ll forever love and Northeast Ohio will always home!"

The 76ers will be the fourth team James has played for in his illustrious career. For Philadelphia, James is the second major star to join the team this offseason, as they acquired Jaylen Brown in a stunning trade with the Boston Celtics earlier this month.

Last season, the 76ers were swept by the New York Knicks in the Eastern Conference semifinals, and they hope the additions of James and Brown can propel them to a championship.

While James may not be the force he once was, he still remains a productive player going into his 24th season. In 60 games with the Los Angeles Lakers last season, James averaged 20.9 points, 7.2 assists and 6.1 rebounds per game. 

Follow Fox News Digital’s sports coverage on X, and subscribe to the Fox News Sports Huddle newsletter.

item.title: Romanian F-16 shoots down drone after airspace breach, marking first for NATO ally
item.description: A Romanian F-16 shot down an unauthorized drone over Buzău County, marking the first such interception in the NATO member's national airspace.
item.content:encoded:
A Romanian F-16 fighter jet shot down a drone that entered the country’s airspace without authorization Friday, marking the first time the NATO member has intercepted and destroyed a drone inside its national airspace, authorities said.

Romanian officials have not yet identified the drone’s origin or purpose. Romania’s army chief said pilots described it as appearing to be a Shahed-type drone, Reuters reported, the Iran-designed attack drone widely used by Russia against Ukraine. 

The incident carries direct implications for the U.S. and its NATO allies, as repeated airspace breaches along the alliance’s eastern flank have fueled concerns that Russia’s war in Ukraine could spill across NATO borders. Romania, which borders Ukraine, has reported dozens of drone incursions or debris incidents since Moscow launched its full-scale invasion in 2022.

Romanian Foreign Minister Oana Țoiu said the interception took place over an unpopulated area in Buzău County.

REPORT WARNS RUSSIA USING SHADOW FLEET TO PROBE NATO DRONE DEFENSES

"Our borders and our sovereignty are non-negotiable, and our military and NATO air police will continue to monitor the land border, the Danube and the Black Sea, as our military frequently identifies risks," Țoiu told Fox News Digital. 

"The impact area and the drone debris are currently being analyzed, but the pattern is consistent with prior confirmed Russian drone flights during the numerous illegal attacks against Ukrainian civilian infrastructure on Romania’s border," she added.

Țoiu said Romania and NATO would continue to defend allied territory.

"NATO works, and we plan to be even stronger with the decisions on air defense and budget allocation from The Hague and Ankara summits," she said. "The path toward increased security is the path toward peace at Romania’s border. We will defend our citizens and we will continue to invest in protecting Romanian and Allied territory."

THE TWO MOST IMPORTANT AMERICAN WEAPONS SYSTEMS NATO IS EAGER TO HAVE

Țoiu previously told Fox News Digital July 2 that Russian drones had been detected inside Ukraine roughly 19 miles from Romania’s border, prompting two Royal Air Force Eurofighter Typhoon jets operating from Romania’s 86th Air Base in Fetești to scramble under NATO’s Enhanced Air Policing mission. A Romanian Air Force IAR 330 Puma helicopter was also deployed, she said at the time.

Romanian President Nicușor Dan and Defense Minister Radu Miruță reaffirmed the country’s commitment to protecting its airspace and investing in modern air-defense and anti-drone capabilities, according to Țoiu.

The incident underscores the growing danger along NATO’s eastern flank as Russia continues strikes across Ukraine and Kyiv increasingly targets Russian military and energy infrastructure deeper inside Russia.

RUSSIA UNLEASHES NEARLY 600 MISSILES AND DRONES ON KYIV IN DEADLIEST STRIKE SINCE MAY

The drone shootdown came as Ukraine reported deadly Russian strikes in the Kyiv region and Slovyansk. 

President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said a rescue operation was underway in the Kyiv region after a Russian missile strike killed six people and wounded dozens. Reuters later reported that Ukrainian officials and industry representatives said the strike killed 10 people and injured close to 100 at a site where a defense industry event was being held.

"There were also casualties in Slovyansk following guided aerial bomb strikes," Zelenskyy said. "Five people were killed. My condolences to their families. Nine others were injured. The Russians damaged ordinary homes, the consulate building, and vehicles."

Latvian Foreign Minister Baiba Braže said Latvia’s honorary consulate building in Slovyansk, in Ukraine’s Donetsk Oblast, was destroyed Friday morning in a Russian attack involving a KAB-250 guided aerial bomb, according to information provided by Ukrainian authorities.

Braže said Latvia’s honorary consul, Oleksandr Pavenko, was not injured, but two consulate staff members were wounded. She informed European Union and NATO foreign ministers and international organizations about the incident, while Latvia summoned Russia’s chargé d’affaires and delivered a note of protest.

"Russia will be held accountable for this and other violations of international law," Latvia’s Foreign Ministry said. "All war crimes committed by Russia must be thoroughly investigated, and those responsible must be brought to justice."

Zelenskyy called the strikes "brutal attacks on civilians" and again pressed Ukraine’s partners for air-defense support.

"Interceptors for Patriot systems are the number one priority," he said.

Ukraine also said its forces had struck one of Russia’s key military enterprises in Kirov, which Zelenskyy said supplies components for Russian aircraft and missile systems used in attacks on Ukrainian cities. Zelenskyy said Ukraine had also hit an oil facility nearly 840 miles away and that operations against Russian logistics supplying drone components, navigation equipment and military gear remained underway. Ukrainian outlet UNN reported that Zelenskyy confirmed the Kirov strike and the long-range strike on the oil facility.

The Security Council is expected to hold an emergency meeting on Ukraine Monday at 10 a.m., as requested by Ukraine, according to a United Nations schedule viewed by Fox News Digital. The meeting comes after Kyiv has repeatedly urged the international community to respond more forcefully to Russia’s attacks on civilians and infrastructure.

The back-and-forth comes as Kyiv is trying to show battlefield momentum despite continued Russian missile and drone attacks. 

Outgoing Ukrainian Commander in Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi said Ukraine had recaptured 270 square miles of territory in 2026 and was handing over to his successor "an army that is not only holding the defence but is also on the offensive," Reuters reported Wednesday. 

Fox News Digital reached out to the Russian defense ministry but didn't receive a response in time for publication.

Reuters contributed to this report.

item.title: Michigan Senate candidate Abdul El-Sayed accused Trump of inciting terrorism over Whitmer kidnapping plot
item.description: Abdul El-Sayed accused Trump of inciting terrorism over the Whitmer kidnapping plot, raising questions as his Michigan Senate primary heats up.
item.content:encoded:
Abdul El-Sayed, the Democratic candidate for Senate in Michigan, once accused President Donald Trump of inciting terrorism in a 2020 interview, citing a foiled plot to kidnap Gov. Gretchen Whitmer.

"These are terrorists; we have to call them that. We have to recognize that that is what the face of terrorism looks like right now. We have to step up against it," El-Sayed said.

"We gotta ask: why are these people getting radicalized? Well, there must be some radical, extremist cleric out there who may be tweeting up a storm about all the things that they think need to be done, like liberating Michigan."

El-Sayed’s comments come as he wages a tight primary race against Rep. Haley Stevens, D-Mich., with the Aug. 4 primary now less than a month away. His remarks also raise questions about his views on political rhetoric and its connection to violence.

MICHIGAN SENATE CANDIDATE ABDUL EL-SAYED DODGES QUESTION ON WHETHER HE STILL SUPPORTS DEFUNDING THE POLICE

His comments reference a foiled plan to kidnap Whitmer that first surfaced in 2020.

A group of men associated with anti-government and militia circles, including the Wolverine Watchmen, allegedly planned to kidnap the governor from her vacation home in northern Michigan in an effort to end her COVID-19 restrictions.

The plot was foiled in October by law enforcement before any attempt was ever made.

Notably, The New York Times later reported that four of the 12 people charged in the alleged plot had been government agents or informants, prompting accusations of entrapment.

Although President Donald Trump did not call for any kind of violence against Whitmer, El-Sayed said he believed the president was at least partially responsible, pointing to a tweet Trump had made six months before the discovered plot.

WATCH: SURFACED VIDEOS OF DEM SENATE CANDIDATE BACKING 'DEFUND THE POLICE' CONTRADICT RECENT DENIALS

"LIBERATE MICHIGAN!" Trump wrote in a post on Twitter, the social media platform now known as X. The post was one of a series calling to "liberate" Virginia and Minnesota from COVID-19 restrictions imposed by Democratic governors. According to FBI comments to local media, the plot originated before Trump's post.

El-Sayed called the posts emblematic of a cultural toxicity that had made violence more likely.

SIGN UP TO GET THE POLITICS NEWSLETTER

"The idea that people would threaten her safety because she was willing to stand up to keep us all healthy is, to me, the example of what the kind of toxicity that we see out of Donald Trump creates," El-Sayed said.

"And right now, the threat we are seeing is radical, far-right White Supremacy and the terrorists who would undo our society’s basic norms and mores."

DSA MEMBER LEADS DEM PRIMARY IN MIDWEST BATTLEGROUND BUT TRAILS LIKELY GOP NOMINEE: POLL

El-Sayed’s campaign did not respond to Fox News Digital's requests for comment on whether his position on Trump's rhetoric had changed in light of The New York Times reporting.

El-Sayed will face off against Stevens in the state’s Aug. 4 primaries.

item.title: Texas Rangers favored over Seattle Mariners as Bryce Miller's road struggles continue in AL West
item.description: Bryce Miller's road struggles and MacKenzie Gore's home dominance shape this American League West betting matchup between Seattle and Texas.
item.content:encoded:
We are in a bit of a dull period for sports. We don't have a ton of the major sports going on at the moment. The NBA has taken a break from Summer League (which set viewership records, by the way). The NFL mini-camps haven't officially started. The NHL does have free agency going on, but most of the bigger names are signed. That leaves us with just baseball, which is fine by me - I love betting on it. Luckily, we have some great games today as well, like this one between the Mariners and Rangers.

The Seattle Mariners are really frustrating because on paper this looks like a team that should be running away with not only their division, but among the best in the American League. That is not the case, unfortunately. The Mariners are 51-52 for the season and have been pretty poor on the road with a 21-29 record. I keep waiting for this team to turn it on, but they are just average. Their offense struggles regularly. The pitching staff is still dominant, but they aren't getting the support needed.

Today the Mariners get arguably the best pitcher in their rotation this season, Bryce Miller, to throw. Miller is 4-4 for the year with a 2.27 ERA and a 0.88 WHIP. The problem for Seattle is that he didn't start the year until May. His ERA by month has also increased every month from 1.71 in May to 2.19 in June, and now 3.06 in July. To be clear, anyone would take those numbers, but it is something to monitor. He has been substantially worse on the road than at home, as well. He has allowed only 16 earned runs this year, but 13 of those have been on the road.

The Texas Rangers are actually leading the American League West at the moment. It isn't a big surprise after they reinvested in their rotation in the offseason. The team doesn't have super elite hitting, but they do still have some very potent bats in the lineup. Their batting average is one of the better ones in the league at .247, but they aren't scoring a ton of runs at just 427 for the season. And that pitching staff they tried to improve is throwing to a 4.32 ERA.

Tonight, the major addition they made in the offseason, MacKenzie Gore, is toeing the rubber. Gore has been fine for the Rangers with a 6-8 record, 4.80 ERA, and a 1.28 WHIP. I do think his WHIP is something that may indicate he can lower his ERA. He has been better at home as well, with a 3.44 ERA. July has been a bit of a rough month for him, with 18 earned runs allowed already, including three of four starts with at least five earned runs allowed. He has faced Seattle twice this season at home; he went five innings, struck out nine hitters, and allowed one hit in the scoreless appearance.

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

I don't love the Mariners in this situation. I know they have the better hitter, but this is a bit of a Jekyll and Hyde situation with Miller. On the road, he is just not nearly as good as at home. Gore hasn't been elite, but he has been much better, and more reliable at home than he has on the road. Seattle has also struggled overall on the road.

For those reasons, I'm taking the Rangers tonight. Gore is a good pitcher, and I do think he can get a win to open this series. Texas is 4-2 against the Mariners this season and already swept them once at home this season. I just think it is a bad situation and spot for the Mariners. I also think that the total goes over 7.5 tonight.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

For more sports betting information and plays, follow David on X/Twitter: @futureprez2024

item.title: US Masters Swimming competitor says her grievance for trans athlete comments has been resolved
item.description: Angie Griffin says she was cleared of unsportsmanlike conduct but remains banned from the U.S. Masters Swimming forum where she advocated policy change.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. Masters Swimming competitor Angie Griffin says she has been cleared of an unsportsmanlike-conduct grievance that left her wondering for eight months whether she would be expelled from the organization, simply for speaking out against trans athletes in women's competitions.

But Griffin says she still cannot access the organization’s online community forum—the same platform where she sought support for changing its transgender-athlete policy.

"I had the actual hearing last month, and in less than a week, I received the resolution, which was that I was not found guilty of unsportsmanlike conduct," Griffin told Fox News Digital.

CAMPUS CONFLICT EXPOSED AT THE CORE OF SJSU'S MESSY FIGHT VS TRUMP ADMIN OVER VOLLEYBALL SCANDAL

"It was eight months of wondering if I was gonna be kicked out of swimming over a grievance that should have never been brought up in the first place."

The grievance stemmed from Griffin’s posts on the USMS community forum, where she advocated for aligning the organization’s eligibility rules more closely with World Aquatics and referred to a transgender competitor as a man.

USMS previously confirmed that an individual member—not the organization itself—filed a grievance related to its code of conduct. Griffin said at the time that the case could have resulted in the removal of her swimming times and honors or her expulsion from USMS.

SJSU VOLLEYBALL SCANDAL PROBE FINDS TEAM ALLEGEDLY RECRUITED MALE FOR ADVANTAGE WITHOUT TELLING FEMALE PLAYERS

That threat is now gone, Griffin said.

Her forum restriction is not.

"Unfortunately, I’m still banned from using the U.S. Masters community forum, which is the only place I can possibly discuss policy change with any other members," Griffin said. "I’m really upset about it because I was cleared of the wrongdoing, and I’m still not allowed back in the one place where members can discuss the policy."

"I’ve been silenced, basically," she added. "The forum’s the only way to reach other members around the country about policy issues."

Griffin emerged as a critic of USMS after competing against a transgender athlete at the organization’s 2025 Spring National Championship in San Antonio. Griffin previously said she did not learn the competitor’s birth sex until after the meet.

The controversy prompted intense scrutiny of the organization’s eligibility rules. USMS introduced an interim policy on July 1, 2025, before revising it again on Feb. 21, 2026.

Under the current policy, swimmers may participate in the competition category corresponding with their gender identity or expression. However, swimmers seeking national recognition in the women’s category—including records, Top 10 rankings and All-American or All-Star honors—must certify that they were female at birth.

Local awards and scoring can be handled differently in jurisdictions with nondiscrimination laws requiring recognition based on gender identity.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

Griffin contends the policy still fails to protect the women’s competition category because it allows biological males to race directly against female swimmers, even when they are ineligible for certain national honors.

She cited a recent Minnesota Masters state championship where she said a transgender athlete swept a women’s age-group category. Griffin said many USMS members remain unaware of how frequently the organization’s policy has changed or what its current rules allow.

"One of my teammates who feels the same way I do about the policy was at a meet down south, and she was shocked that so many members, women and men alike, had no idea that they had changed the policy three times since last year, or that they were even still allowing men to swim against women," Griffin said.

item.title: Oklahoma state agency fires staff attorney after anti-ICE social media tirade
item.description: Shannon Desherow was fired after an internal probe into her expletive-filled TikTok video encouraging resistance to federal immigration enforcement.
item.content:encoded:
The Oklahoma State Bureau of Investigation has fired a staff attorney after an internal investigation into an anti-ICE social media tirade that prompted widespread criticism, the agency confirmed to Fox News Digital Friday.

"Once we were made aware of her social media post, we opened an internal investigation," a spokesperson for the Oklahoma State Bureau of Investigation told Fox News Digital in a statement Friday. "Upon completion of the internal investigation, she was terminated. Since this is a personnel matter, we are unable to further comment at this time."

The termination follows Fox News Digital's earlier reporting that the fired staff attorney, Shannon Desherow, had been placed under investigation after posting an expletive-filled video criticizing Immigration and Customs Enforcement and encouraging resistance to federal immigration enforcement.

DOJ PUTS BLUE STATES ON NOTICE AS ICE FIGHT BARRELS TOWARD NEXT CONSTITUTIONAL SHOWDOWN

In a TikTok video reshared by Libs of TikTok on July 9, Desherow said, "Conservatives really think they’re doing something when they say, ‘Just comply’ when it comes to ICE and United States citizens."

"And it just shows that they really just f---ing chirp with no understanding of what they’re talking about, because why the f--- would I comply with someone who has no jurisdiction over me," she asked in the video.

Desherow continued, "ICE has no authority and no jurisdiction over U.S. citizens. So why the f--- would I comply with an order given to me at gunpoint by somebody who has no f---ing authority to make that order? I’m not going to. That’s the f---ing thing."

WISCONSIN TEACHER FIRED AFTER SOCIAL MEDIA POST ADVOCATING TO 'MAKE AMERICANS GREAT ASSASSINS AGAIN'

On her LinkedIn page, Desherow still says she’s "Currently working for the OSBI" as assistant general counsel. Her TikTok page had been set to private but appears to be public again.

Her bio appears to include a Palestinian flag emoji and reads, "Lawyer, mom, bookworm, leftist in a red state (views are entirely my own and not representative of my employer)."

Desherow, according to her LinkedIn, graduated in December 2016 from the University of Central Oklahoma and then received her law degree from the University of Oklahoma College of Law in 2020.

DOJ ESCALATES BLUE-STATE ICE STANDOFF AFTER STATES REFUSE KEY FEDERAL REQUEST

"And an ICE warrant, by the way, 99% of the time, is an administrative warrant. It’s not good enough for the f---ing Fourth Amendment," Desherow said. "It doesn’t give you access to my house, it doesn’t give you access to my car, and it certainly doesn’t give you access to f---ing arrest me. I was born here, and I’m going to make it an ICE agent’s problem. I don’t have to f---ing comply with ICE, because I was born here. And every citizen needs to have that f---ing mindset. We need to make their lives f---ing miserable, and that’s exactly what we should be doing."

In a pinned video on her TikTok page, Desherow said, "I don’t want to hear a single conservative in my comment section chirping about Sharia law because of Zohran's [Mamdani] election. OK, you guys love Sharia law, you love fundamentalist extremism when White people are implementing it."

She also made critical TikToks about Charlie Kirk following his assassination, including one where she said, "Charlie Kirk inherited the society that he helped create. Charlie Kirk and other conservative talking heads like him preach stochastic violence endlessly and then whine and cry when they’re the victim of their own f---ing violent rhetoric."

Fox News Digital reached out to ICE and attempted to reach Desherow for comment.

item.title: WATCH: Scalise serves up Olive Garden reality check for Democrats in voter ID fight
item.description: Steve Scalise compares Olive Garden's photo ID requirement for its unlimited pasta pass to the lack of a federal voter ID law in U.S. elections.
item.content:encoded:
House Majority Leader Steve Scalise, R-La., is comparing the lack of a federal voter ID law to an identification requirement at one of America's most well-known chain restaurants.

"If you want to go buy unlimited pasta at the Olive Garden, they said you have to show your ID," Scalise told Fox News Digital in an interview earlier this week.

He also pointed out that the 2024 Democratic National Convention (DNC) required attendees to show ID in order to enter the venue.

SEN MARK WARNER CALLED OUT IN ABC INTERVIEW FOR INACCURATE VOTER ID CLAIMS

"Democrats are saying it somehow impedes people's ability to participate, yet Democrats themselves wouldn't even let you go to their convention without showing ID," Scalise said. "I'm not against them having to require ID – but by the way, everybody understands it, and everybody gets it in polling. You know, if you're Black, if you're White, if you are Hispanic, if you're Republican, or Democrat, or Independent, you understand that an ID makes sense because it protects your right to vote."

He also listed boarding a plane and buying "a six-pack of beer" as situations where a photo ID was required as well.

"How about the franchise of American democracy? Let's make sure you are who you say you are and illegals can't vote in American elections," Scalise said.

TRUMP INTERVENES AS HOUSE GOP REVOLT PUTS SAVE AMERICA ACT AT CENTER OF SPENDING SHOWDOWN

Republicans have emphasized polling showing broad support for voter ID requirements as their main argument against Democrats' opposition to the Safeguarding American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) America Act, which was advanced by the House multiple times but has stalled in the Senate.

Democrats have argued the election integrity package is tantamount to voter suppression, claiming it would make it harder for married women who have changed their names to vote and make absentee voting more burdensome. Another provision in SAVE America that's angering Democrats would allow the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) comprehensive access to states' voter rolls.

But Republicans say that's not true. The SAVE America Act, among other provisions, would mandate proof of citizenship in the voter registration process and implement a federal photo ID requirement to vote in U.S. elections.

WHITE HOUSE DISHES OUT NEW ELECTION SECURITY JAB OVER OLIVE GARDEN'S PASTA PASS ID POLICY

And Republicans have seized on a temporary promotion at the Italian-American restaurant chain Olive Garden as the latest example of the commonsense reasoning behind photo ID.

Earlier this month, the restaurant chain unveiled a "Never-ending pasta pass," which would give 10,000 customers unlimited pasta for dine-in visits across 13 weeks, for the price of $100.

The Olive Garden website states that "Passholders must present a photo I.D. along with Pass at time of ordering" to ensure one pass is not being shared by multiple people.

House Administration Committee Chairman Bryan Steil, R-Wis., referenced the promotion during debate on a separate voter ID measure earlier this week, which Democrats also opposed.

"The never ending pasta bowl is back at Olive Garden. I used to work at Olive Garden, so this hit close to home. But to safeguard the integrity of their pasta program, Olive Garden, as you may have seen, implemented photo ID requirements," Steil said. "However, in jurisdictions across the country from Illinois and California or Minnesota, an individual can go to the polls on Election Day and cast a ballot without providing confirmation, without providing confirmation of their identity."

That measure, which would require photo voter ID even when sending mail-in ballots, passed the House earlier this week with the help of just 13 Democrats.

item.title: Illinois worker spent over six hours trapped in gas station sign too tall for fire truck ladders to reach
item.description: AJ Gow spent over six hours trapped inside a 130-foot gas station sign in Wilmington, Illinois, after his boom lift malfunctioned during repair work.
item.content:encoded:
An Illinois maintenance worker was trapped for more than six hours in a 130-foot-tall gas station sign after his lift malfunctioned.

The worker, who identified himself as AJ Gow, was doing repair work Tuesday on the towering sign, which is located along Lorenzo Road off Interstate 55 in Wilmington.

At 1:55 p.m., the Wilmington Fire District was called to this area on a report of a worker stuck inside one of the enclosed signs atop the sign pole.

EIGHT RIDERS LEFT DANGLING ATOP 100-FOOT ROLLER COASTER FOR OVER THREE HOURS AT TEXAS AMUSEMENT PARK

"The telescopic boom lift used by the worker had malfunctioned and Fire District personnel were told he had been at the elevated position for around 3 hours," officials with the fire district said in a statement on social media.

Additionally, the sign pole had no stairs or ladders built into it, making it impossible for rescue teams to climb up without another lift, officials said.

Another problem was that the Wilmington Fire District's trucks have ladders that only go 100 feet in the air, which meant Gow was 30 feet out of reach.

Eventually, fire officials located another boom lift in the Joliet area, which is roughly 20 miles north of the rest stop. The lift was brought to the sign with an Illinois State Police escort, officials said.

FAMILY'S SPRING BREAK HIKE TURNS INTO LIFE-OR-DEATH RESCUE AFTER PARENT FALLS 70 FEET OFF UTAH CLIFF

After the new lift was in place, firefighters were able to reach Gow and get him back to the ground, the fire district said.

Gow thanked all the first responders from different departments that came to his aid, saying that "seeing that lift roll up with police escorts was the highlight of my day for sure!!"

Gow later said on social media that he spent over six hours in a cramped position within the interior of the sign.

"I was too hot and thirsty to be anxious. I was moreso nauseous and dizzy. And incredibly uncomfortable to be in for over 6 hours. It was only 16" wide and wasn't even tall enough to stand fully upright either," Gow wrote in response to someone commenting on the fire district's post.

In another comment, Gow said the sign "kept swaying with the wind."

Gow said that after he got down, he went to the hospital to be treated for dehydration. Fox News Digital reached out to Gow for comment.

item.title: Newsom says film tax incentives are bringing Hollywood home but industry experts warn it may be 'too late'
item.description: Hollywood experts tell Fox News Digital the Los Angeles film industry needs a "major rescue" after years of studios fleeing for cheaper production hubs despite new tax credits.
item.content:encoded:
California Gov. Gavin Newsom says Hollywood is returning to its glory after years of production fleeing Los Angeles for cheaper filming locations, but industry experts tell Fox News Digital the sector is still reeling from production "decimation."

"We need a major rescue, like, immediately," Shadowcast Pictures owner Jay Ellison said.

Major studios are increasingly opting to film television shows and movies outside California, such as Canada and overseas in the United Kingdom, Ireland and Australia, as well as to domestic production hubs in Georgia and New Mexico.

Paramount Skydance CEO David Ellison is also reportedly considering relocating Paramount’s headquarters — which could divert billions of production dollars from California — while production of scripted television shows in Los Angeles is down nearly 30% since 2019, according to Luminate data.

JOE ROGAN MOCKS GAVIN NEWSOM FOR DISMISSING GROWING STATE PROBLEMS AS LA FILM PRODUCTION CRATERS
